findings.evidence: `rg -n "References|TensorProduct\.antipode_def|HopfAlgebra\.TensorProduct"` shows the file imports `Mathlib.RingTheory.HopfAlgebra.TensorProduct` at line 7 and uses `simp [TensorProduct.antipode_def]` at line 132, while the references at lines 35-36 name only `Mathlib.RingTheory.Bialgebra.TensorProduct` and `AlgHom.liftEquiv`.
findings.fix: Extend the references to credit `Mathlib.RingTheory.HopfAlgebra.TensorProduct`, specifically `TensorProduct.antipode_def`, alongside the existing bialgebra tensor-product and `AlgHom.liftEquiv` credit.
findings.issue: The `## References` block omits the central source for the new Hopf-level result, even though `baseChange_antipode_tmul` is proved directly from `TensorProduct.antipode_def`.

summary: The new Hopf base-change API relies centrally on Mathlib's Hopf tensor-product file but the in-code references still credit only the bialgebra tensor-product file and `AlgHom.liftEquiv`. Add the Hopf tensor-product source to the module references.
